rationale: Lovett et al. (2013) is cited multiple times for specific roles, not just as background: as an exemplar of prior work on what generates WOM volume, as evidence on online versus offline talk patterns, and as prior brand-property-to-WOM evidence in the paper's brand-level extension discussion. These citations help define the citing paper's research gap and position its contribution as moving from WOM volume antecedents to conversation-level WOM impact on recipient intentions. The citing paper does not directly reuse the cited model as its main method, so the tie is substantive but primarily a positioning/complement relation.
relation_description: The citing paper uses the cited study as a key benchmark in the WOM literature and then shifts focus from brand-level WOM generation to conversation-level consequences (purchase and retransmission intentions). It also leverages the cited study's brand/channel findings to motivate interpretation and a supplemental brand-level analysis.
